freshnessTargets: Shift from 'finding forgotten objects' to a small, forward-looking action
Use weather as active texture (drizzle on surfaces, cool air) rather than just reflective support
narrativeNudges: Frame a tiny errand or preparation as a quiet promise to tomorrow
captionAdvice: Start with a sensory detail of the late-evening drizzle or cool air
End with a gentle, forward-looking thought, not a completion
imageAdvice: Focus on a small action in motion (e.g., hands adjusting, stepping out)
Use the cool, damp light of early spring evening
summary: After many quiet rediscoveries, suggest a small, forward-leaning action in the cool drizzle to gently refresh the narrative.
